summary: Iteration 81 - RETEST of bug fix from iteration 80. Table audit logging now works correctly. All 24 tests passed (100%). The fix added _log_audit calls directly to table endpoints in server.py (lines 4064-4180).
critical_code_review_comments: Bug fix verified: PUT /api/caisse/tables/{id} with items now creates audit log entity_type='table' action='update'
Bug fix verified: DELETE /api/caisse/tables/{id}?reason=cancelled now creates audit log entity_type='table' action='delete'
Cleanup exclusion verified: DELETE without reason does NOT create log (post-invoice cleanup)
Cleanup exclusion verified: PUT with status='invoiced' + items=[] does NOT create log (post-invoice cleanup)
No regression on invoice audit logs - all 22 invoice tests still pass
seed_data_creation: Test tables and invoices created and cleaned up during tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 81 confirmed bug fix from iteration 80. Table audit logging now works correctly. The fix was implemented by adding _log_audit calls directly to the existing table endpoints in server.py (lines 4064-4180) rather than including the separate tables.py router. Key behaviors: (1) PUT with items creates 'update' log with items diff summary, (2) DELETE with reason=cancelled creates 'delete' log, (3) DELETE without reason does NOT log (cleanup), (4) PUT with status='invoiced' + items=[] does NOT log (post-invoice cleanup). All 24 audit log tests pass.

bugs_fixed_in_this_iteration.bug: PUT /api/caisse/tables/{id} did not create audit log
DELETE /api/caisse/tables/{id}?reason=cancelled did not create audit log
